Hacker News Confidence

Less annoying hacker news. (What is this?)

This site scrapes Hacker News once every 30 minutes, then sorts according to this formula, with upvotes as positive signals and comments as negative ones. In my experience, the best articles to read on HN are the ones with a high upvote-to-discussion ratio. Mostly because controversial pieces tend to produce a disproportionate number of comments compared to upvotes.


  1. Megadev: A Development Kit for the Sega Mega Drive and Mega CD Hardware 96 upvotes, 5 comments
  2. Show HN: GitAgent – An open standard that turns any Git repo into an AI agent 51 upvotes, 3 comments
  3. Baochip-1x: What it is, why I'm doing it now and how it came about 228 upvotes, 29 comments
  4. Nmap in the movies (2008) 111 upvotes, 15 comments
  5. Can I run AI locally? 1371 upvotes, 325 comments
  6. Sunsetting Jazzband 62 upvotes, 13 comments
  7. Cookie jars capture American kitsch (2023) 19 upvotes, 2 comments
  8. I found 39 Algolia admin keys exposed across open source documentation sites 151 upvotes, 45 comments
  9. Generalizing Knuth's Pseudocode Architecture to Knowledge 9 upvotes, 0 comments
  10. Show HN: Ink – Deploy full-stack apps from AI agents via MCP or Skills 22 upvotes, 3 comments
  11. Python: The Optimization Ladder 195 upvotes, 64 comments
  12. 1M context is now generally available for Opus 4.6 and Sonnet 4.6 1062 upvotes, 448 comments
  13. Philosoph Jürgen Habermas Gestorben 123 upvotes, 42 comments
  14. Show HN: Learn Arabic with spaced repetition and comprehensible input 29 upvotes, 7 comments
  15. The Isolation Trap: Erlang 130 upvotes, 56 comments
  16. Everything you never wanted to know about visually-hidden 19 upvotes, 5 comments
  17. Online astroturfing: A problem beyond disinformation 62 upvotes, 28 comments
  18. RAM kits are now sold with one fake RAM stick alongside a real one 204 upvotes, 141 comments
  19. An ode to bzip 46 upvotes, 25 comments
  20. Montana passes Right to Compute act (2025) 191 upvotes, 136 comments
  21. Nominal Types in WebAssembly 31 upvotes, 15 comments
  22. Hostile Volume – A game about adjusting volume with intentionally bad UI 13 upvotes, 5 comments
  23. What happens when US economic data becomes unreliable 230 upvotes, 203 comments
  24. XML Is a Cheap DSL 201 upvotes, 202 comments
  25. Digg is gone again 364 upvotes, 386 comments
  26. The $2 per hour worker behind the OnlyFans boom 71 upvotes, 63 comments
  27. In Praise of Stupid Questions 6 upvotes, 2 comments
  28. Wired headphone sales are exploding 367 upvotes, 619 comments
  29. Show HN: KeyID – Free email and phone infrastructure for AI agents (MCP) 5 upvotes, 6 comments
  30. 9 Mothers Defense (YC P26) Is Hiring in Austin 0 upvotes, 0 comments
